title: TM-Controlled DBMS Connectors

TM-Controlled DBMS Connectors

TM-Controlled DBMS Connectors

A database connector can be controlled by a transaction manager only if it is TM-capable. Uniface does not provide TM-controlled connectors and does not directly support third-party transaction managers. However, you can use the APIs provided by the Database Connector Interface to build a custom database connector that does.

Connectors indicate whether or not they support this feature via the uinfo->TMCapable in the information request. TM-controlled DBMS connectors cannot initiate begin, commit or rollback transactions.

Connectors that provide TM support can be included in either local or global (TM-controlled) transactions. This is determined at run time. If the DBMS connector path is included in the paths within a TM section of the server assignment file, the connector is TM-controlled.

Requirements for TM-Controlled DBMS Connectors

The following is a list of requirements for TM-controlled connectors:

  • The DBMS must support the XA (X/Open DTP model) protocol.
  • The connector must not connect to or disconnect from the DBMS even when the connector receives Logon and Logoff requests. These requests are provided so that the connector can perform any necessary internal administration and resource management. The transaction manager connects to and disconnects from the DBMS.
  • The DBMS connector must not begin any local transactions after the connector logon request is completed.
  • Uniface does not issue any DBMS connector commit or rollback requests. Therefore, TM-controlled connectors cannot perform resource management and internal administration during these requests.
  • The connector must support the Global Transaction, (‘G’) request.
  • Cursors may not remain open after a transaction has ended. Therefore, the DBMS must be configured to implicitly close all open cursors during global commits and rollbacks.
  • If the connector sets a time-out for locks, it must be greater than or equal to the value set by the transaction manager.
  • The DBMS connector must be on a server which is controlled by a transaction manager.

Starting Global Transactions

Global transactions are started on a ProcScript command activate call. Global transactions are also started on data manipulation in a component with TX mode set to Requires new transaction in the Uniface Signature Editor.

Considerations for a TM-Controlled Connector

To keep track of global transactions, the following types of information are required:

  • Location of services in server processes
  • Mapping of service requests to server processes
  • The client process from which a service request originated

Depending upon the database connector, you need to take the following factors into account:

  • Locking—check if the behavior differs between local and global transactions.
  • Isolation level—this may differ between local and global transactions.
  • Multiplexing—multiple DBMS connector connections may be multiplexed into one DBMS connection. The DBMS connector is not be aware of this situation unless it can query the DBMS for a connection identifier. Connectors which perform internal administration based on path ID could have problems.
Related Links
  • uinfo

    Supplies Uniface with basic information about the DBMS and the connector.
